textByHash.c3ca9f97c7c306cdc7f906de0e3b5f5456ff2ac9: Now is the time you die.
textByHash.1c681dcdf6e7162db866a69b46424ce1d31d456b: Beware mortals, now travels with me.
textByHash.77cc2167aa6236d8f63e952590aa630422a906e5: Hello mortal. Have you come to retrieve something you lost when you died?
textByHash.ccecc791576e996e45426452b3f858802cbc0d7e: When you die, you often drop things you were carrying. In most cases, a gravestone appears near where you died, and your items can be retrieved there.
textByHash.b60258955dd8dfb3c177d5afc078a9543f936174: However, gravestones do not last for long. When your gravestone collapses, your items come here, to me. I can return them to you - though I charge a fee for my services.
textByHash.39de5a71cbe7e2de3acab74c8536b59f7aec4648: Aside from that, if you bring me your unwanted possessions, and sacrifice them into my Coffer here, I'll count their value against any future fees I charge you.
textByHash.63c619aca1b008e5c9ddb94eb4154f2eeaf5f3a4: I don't accept just any junk, but I'll try to give you a good price for anything decent.
textByHash.af4a6ef2c9cfb8364dbc67f4397299d204c43912: So do you need to retrieve something you lost when you died?
textByHash.029a142895c1bb41ffa2ed98ca9c3a5c347fc43d: As you should be able to see, I am Death. The Grim Reaper. The Inexorable One. The Inescapable Grasp of Mortal Destiny. And this is my little office. Nice, isn't it?
textByHash.e55050aea5ac4f603ef443d32197d657be6ed664: Thank you. I feel the colour scheme suits my style. Now, can I help you retrieve something you lost when you died?
textByHash.08d811529147b30cc1dc6e577657ecb2d79bfdc2: Sometimes it's hard to care for beauty when one's very existence is just seen as a curse upon the world. Now, do you actually want to retrieve something from me?
textByHash.42d67b07d6708a9a42bc7bf60997ec6b34066776: You haven't got 200,000 coins to pay for that. I can take coins from your inventory, or from my Coffer.
textByHash.ca3d2c9895a563b5d603171a0403fb5565d0ffb4: Most people won't see your grave, by the way. It appears to you, but it aims to be invisible to everyone else. Are you sure you want to buy a cosmetic override for it?
textByHash.3486145c23950af1b3ed3bde2de3fc126a6801d2: It is not for me to judge how people spend their money.
textByHash.f37522bca8a4b7dd88dca844176f1dee18e0b121: Do you really want to switch to the basic gravestone? I'm not offering to give back the fee you paid earlier.
textByHash.7e3fe4591e224a7e65ff09fe2f75a7d6dfd333d8: You've already got the basic gravestone.
textByHash.94ef6a014201b509f22166b42ef6bba59a35d867: How would you like to... die?
textByHash.2e17fb689359223492bc706b29a864f2e1a34500: Really? There isn't much else for you to do here. But you know your business.
textByHash.d337a4670cc2d98cf42f975993ecd9710871d62d: Hello mortal. If you're looking for something you lost when you died, I'm afraid I don't cater to Ultimate Ironmen. Your items would have fallen on the ground where you died.
textByHash.3cf82db017eee7d736fb2e00add862c9fa3dce68: So, mortal... you have died, as all mortals do. As you have suffered the loss of some items on this occasion, I have brought you here to explain for you how that works.
textByHash.7fb72884780f1d7c22b0f821198aaf458447ce8e: Let me explain... Across most of the land of Gielinor, when you die, some of the items you're carrying are dropped.
textByHash.0c9734cd08282fe31e345aaab41ddef36aefd5bc: I will look after such items, without a time limit. However, I do charge more for my services. It would be cheaper to retrieve them from the gravestone directly, if you can reach it in time.
textByHash.481d3b2ff7fdaf1df925e8002bad5d7b784b1307: Gladly... Across most of the land of Gielinor, when you die, some of the items you're carrying are dropped.
textByHash.3ea1d7f1db7c5b6f61791a9c7f242d2cc5f3f5d0: Cash is always acceptable. Also, a gravestone can take it directly from you bank, in case you didn't have enough with you.
textByHash.7509d784121f10f74719ad163fd35a9c49f75eda: Besides that, take a look at my Coffer here. You can bring your unwanted possessions, and sacrifice them into my Coffer. I'll count their value against any future gravestone fees you're charged.
textByHash.6820dc939aa37b94e83f7df08ea79430cf085822: There is a menu to predict these things for you. Take a look at your Worn Items side-panel...
textByHash.0b33daa8ced4dfcc5bee6a0ef0324ee0c26a2d0f: I'm afraid we have more topics to cover before you leave. Speak to me when you are ready to continue.
textByHash.87bc4e3518b1f398ed797ad9cf14df2935dc6947: Very well. Once you pass through my portal, you will have 15 minutes to reach your gravestone.
textByHash.647295de874770fc3a46c333893466f8647c82f7: I believe I was explaining gravestones to you. We still have some more topics to cover.
textByHash.2cc5894a43390d69a22e11427c1cff01c50aa95b: I haven't finished talking to you yet. Please don't worry about your gravestone - it will wait until you leave this place.
textByHash.2a056825184af2faa740853586c4d6eeb852637d: Would you like me to remind you about gravestones?
textByHash.d003295ffef1d2b3e62e5ca0be1e45e85962084b: You haven't unlocked that. You can do so by reading a Trailblazer reloaded death scroll.
textByHash.ee210aed3574d670cf94bf2322d0fe5f3f3aebf5: Have you come to retrieve something you lost from a previous gravestone?
textByHash.08a09515769429c7309c684e76401abc405cd688: You will now die like a Trailblazer.
textByHash.12fed0f4138e3ea946c9c2bd1c3e20ee8f9937a0: You will now perish like most others in Gielinor.
textByHash.1e3076c1260d6138c3eda16d6c97d21f55025d59: Hello mortal. If you're looking for something you lost when you died, I'm afraid I don't cater to Ultimate Iron Men. Your items would have fallen on the ground where you died.
textByHash.0bd08381a9218559b18de667e43eaa5fd5b18606: Hello mortal. If you're looking for something you lost when you died, I'm afraid I don't cater to Ultimate Iron Women. Your items would have fallen on the ground where you died.
textByHash.c5aa87741a8bd547a8ffaccd73169301ea53d856: Nothing, really. Please leave when you are ready.
